stories.headline: Denver homicides jump 44% in first half of 2026; six children among those killed
stories.summary:
Nearly one in six people shot or killed in Denver during the first half of 2026 were children, reversing safety gains the city had seen over the previous two years. The city recorded 26 homicides in the first six months of the year, compared to 18 at the same point in 2025, when Denver posted the third-lowest annual homicide total since 1990.

Six children were killed and 11 more were wounded in shootings through June 30. By comparison, no children had been killed by July 1 in 2025, and just two were wounded. Denver Police Chief Ron Thomas said personal disputes, social media conflicts and a loosely organized youth gang element are driving much of the violence.

Two of the six child fatalities are suspected victims of abuse, including a 2-month-old girl whose death is still under investigation and a 2-year-old girl whose mother and mother's boyfriend have been charged. The other four victims were teenagers between 14 and 17 years old, including a 14-year-old boy shot inside the Montbello Recreation Center.

Despite the homicide increase, police data shows other crime categories — including property crime, auto theft and burglary — are down compared to the same period in 2025. Thomas noted that overall homicide levels remain well below the pandemic-era peak, when 50 people had been killed by July 1, 2022.

stories.headline: Denver hits 100 degrees for first time in 2026 as heat advisory blankets Front Range
stories.summary:
Denver officially cracked 100 degrees for the first time in 2026 on Monday, with the National Weather Service recording the milestone at approximately 2:52 p.m. at Denver International Airport. The temperature fell short of the all-time July record of 105 degrees set on the same date in 2005.

The NWS issued a Heat Advisory for Denver and surrounding counties — including Adams, Arapahoe, Boulder, Broomfield, Douglas, Jefferson, Larimer and Weld — through 9 p.m. Monday. The agency classified the heat risk at Level 3 out of 4, warning of major impacts for anyone without access to cooling or adequate hydration.

Colorado's Department of Public Health and Environment also flagged elevated ozone levels in the Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups category across the Denver metro area, advising those with respiratory conditions to reduce prolonged outdoor activity through at least 4 p.m. Temperatures were expected to remain in the low-to-mid 90s Tuesday and Wednesday, with afternoon rain chances offering some relief.

stories.headline: I-70 closes in both directions in Garfield County after mudslide triggered by flash flooding
stories.summary:
A mudslide triggered by flash flooding forced the closure of Interstate 70 in both directions in Garfield County on Monday evening, disrupting travel along the major east-west corridor through the mountains. The closure snarled traffic in the area as drivers sought alternate routes.

The incident adds to a series of weather-related road disruptions along Colorado's mountain corridors this summer, with heavy monsoon moisture fueling flash flooding and instability on hillsides. Garfield County is located along a stretch of I-70 that runs through canyon terrain particularly vulnerable to debris flows.

No additional details on duration of the closure or reports of injuries were immediately available. Travelers were advised to check CDOT's road conditions portal before heading into the area.

stories.headline: BP subsidiary proposes Colorado's largest solar farm on 2,800 acres near Denver International Airport
stories.summary:
Lightsource BP has submitted a proposal to develop the Big Bronco Solar project, a 2,800-acre solar farm near Denver International Airport in Adams County that would become the largest solar installation in Colorado if approved. The project includes a battery storage component designed to store energy generated by the facility.

The proposal must clear state regulatory review before construction can proceed. Adams County land near DIA has emerged as a focus for large-scale renewable energy development given its flat terrain and proximity to transmission infrastructure.

If built, Big Bronco Solar would significantly expand Colorado's solar capacity, which has grown substantially in recent years as utilities and developers move to meet state renewable energy mandates. Lightsource BP is a global solar developer majority-owned by the British energy giant BP.

stories.headline: Denver Tech Center office-to-apartment conversion yields area's first affordable residential units of its kind
stories.summary:
A former office building in the Denver Tech Center has been converted into residential apartments, marking one of the first completed office-to-residential conversions in Denver since the pandemic and the first such project in the area to include affordable units developed by a private developer. Shea Properties led the conversion, with income-qualified residents eligible for lower rents based on limits set by the Colorado Housing and Finance Authority.

The project illustrates both the opportunity and the difficulty of adaptive reuse in Denver's commercial real estate market. Office-to-residential conversions are widely cited as a potential tool to address the city's housing shortage while reducing vacant commercial space, but they are costly and logistically complex, often requiring extensive structural work to accommodate residential plumbing and ventilation.

City officials and housing advocates have pointed to conversions as one component of Denver's broader affordability strategy, though the number of completed projects remains limited. The DTC building's completion is expected to draw attention from other developers evaluating similar projects.

stories.headline: Denver's River North Brewery earns second-place overall finish and Brewery of the Year title at U.S. Open Beer Championship
stories.summary:
River North Brewery in Denver took home six medals — including four gold — at the 2026 U.S. Open Beer Championship, placing second overall in the global competition and earning the Brewery of the Year designation for producers making between 1,001 and 2,000 barrels per year. Colorado breweries collectively captured 30 medals in the Ohio-based contest, a slight increase from 29 in 2025.

River North's gold-medal beers included Bucket of Bolts (strong ale), Nightmare Fuel (dark coffee beer), Single Cask Adrift on the Baltic (barrel-aged German lager/Baltic porter), and Matt's Secret Stache (extreme beer). The brewery also earned silver and bronze recognition in the imperial porter and extreme beer categories, respectively.

Denver's Bull & Bush Brewery also claimed six medals — one gold, two silver and three bronze. Other Colorado honorees included Liquid Mechanics Brewing Co. in Lafayette, Old 121 Brewhouse in Lakewood, Gilded Goat Brewing Co. in Fort Collins, and Vail Brewing Co., each earning two medals. The strong showing continues Colorado's reputation as one of the country's premier craft brewing states.

stories.headline: Denver's IMAX 70mm projector at Regal Colorado Center fails on opening weekend of Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'
stories.summary:
The IMAX 70mm projector at the Regal UA Colorado Center in Denver malfunctioned during the opening weekend of Christopher Nolan's film "The Odyssey," leaving ticketholders frustrated and forcing the theater to cancel or convert all remaining 70mm showings to a standard digital IMAX format. The Regal theater is one of only 25 venues in the country and one of two in Colorado equipped with the rare 70mm format.

The projector went down during an 11:40 a.m. Saturday showing, stopping the film approximately three minutes in. Theater staff spent roughly 40 minutes attempting a repair before canceling the showing and offering refunds. Some moviegoers who had purchased tickets weeks or months in advance were not notified of the format change until they arrived at the theater, and others stood in long refund lines in temperatures exceeding 90 degrees.

The Cinemark Carefree Circle XD theater in Colorado Springs — the state's other 70mm-capable venue — also experienced a projector issue Saturday when the film was torn during a showing, though that theater's projector was repaired later in the day. As of Monday morning, the Regal projector remained down, with IMAX reportedly flying in parts from Canada to complete the fix.

"The Odyssey" is the first feature film shot entirely on IMAX cameras. Nolan has described the 70mm format as the "best possible experience" for audiences, offering unparalleled sharpness and a screen that fills the viewer's peripheral vision.

stories.headline: Denver investor uses civil courts to wrest neglected properties from absentee owners, filling gap left by city
stories.summary:
A Denver investor named John Crays has developed a practice of convincing neighbors of neglected or derelict properties to assign their private right of action — a tool established under city ordinance — to him, enabling him to pursue court-appointed caretakers for troubled buildings through the civil court system. Crays, working with a housing attorney, has begun systematically targeting properties on Denver's neglected and derelict list that the city itself has been slow to address.

Denver officials acknowledge the city has moved for court-appointed caretakers only four times since the start of 2025, and two of those actions came only after Crays filed his own lawsuits. City officials say they face constitutional constraints and limited bandwidth in the city attorney's office when it comes to compelling property owners to remediate or surrender problem properties.

Supporters say Crays is solving a real problem for residents who have lived for years next to abandoned buildings used by squatters and drug users. Critics, including nonprofit housing attorneys, warn that the tools being deployed are among the most legally coercive available and that private investor control of such proceedings could lead to inequitable outcomes for communities.

Crays said his interest in the issue dates back 15 years to when he encountered a neglected property in his Baker neighborhood. He frames his work as filling a gap the city has failed to address, though questions remain about whether a community-oriented ownership model would better serve long-term neighborhood interests.

stories.headline: Nuggets weigh whether Peyton Watson is worth $25 million per season amid restricted free agency uncertainty
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets' offseason has narrowed to a central question: whether to retain restricted free agent Peyton Watson at a salary that could reach $25 million per season or pursue a sign-and-trade to recoup value. Watson, 23, averaged 14.6 points per game last season and shot 41.1% from three-point range, and stepped up to average 22.8 points over 16 games when Nikola Jokic missed time with a knee injury.

Analysts have pegged Watson's market value between $14 million and $25 million annually, with his camp arguing he should exceed the salaries of teammates Christian Braun ($21.55 million) and Cam Johnson ($23 million) given superior production. The Nuggets are operating near the NBA's second apron salary threshold, which limits their flexibility and has contributed to a quiet offseason.

The Atlanta Hawks' acquisition of Lu Dort has reduced the pool of potential sign-and-trade suitors for Watson, narrowing leverage for both sides. Agent Rich Paul is working to generate outside interest, but the Nuggets retain the right to match any offer sheet as Watson's restricted free agent status gives Denver the final say.

Denver's front office has yet to make a significant offseason addition around Jokic, drawing scrutiny from observers who note the window for contention around the two-time MVP grows more uncertain each year without roster improvement.

stories.headline: Colorado Avalanche sign forward Georgii Merkulov to one-year contract for 2026-27 season
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche announced Monday the signing of forward Georgii Merkulov to a one-year contract for the 2026-27 season. Merkulov, 25, spent the 2025-26 season with the AHL's Providence Bruins, where he recorded 61 points — 24 goals and 37 assists — in 70 games, leading the team in both assists and points on a club that won the AHL's best regular-season record.

Merkulov appeared in one NHL game with the Boston Bruins last season and has 11 career NHL games to his name across three seasons with the Boston organization. He has compiled 240 points in 271 regular-season AHL games and was named to the AHL All-Rookie Team in 2022-23. The 5-foot-11 Russian native was a former AHL All-Star Game selection and was teammates with Fabian Lysell, whom the Avalanche acquired in a trade on June 27.

The signing reflects Colorado's approach to filling roster depth on a tight salary cap following a Western Conference Finals exit in May. General manager Joe Sakic has prioritized affordable additions with upside as the team retools around its core of Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar and Gabriel Landeskog.

stories.headline: Rockies' pitching prospects to watch in second half as rotation posts worst ERA in modern baseball history
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rockies' offensive turnaround under president of baseball operations Paul DePodesta has been tangible in the second half of 2026, with the team ranked in the top 10 in the majors in batting average, OPS and on-base percentage entering a homestand against the Washington Nationals. The pitching rotation, however, remains a severe liability, with a 5.98 ERA ranking last in the majors in fielding independent pitching and 28th in wins above replacement.

Five minor league starters have emerged as prospects to track: left-hander Konner Eaton, a sixth-round pick out of George Mason in 2024 who posted a 3.78 ERA in 14 Double-A starts this season; right-hander Jordy Vargas, a 2021 Dominican Republic signee returning from Tommy John surgery with a 5.68 ERA at High-A Spokane; and right-hander Jackson Cox, also working back from Tommy John surgery.

Eaton, who was the organization's minor league pitcher of the year in 2025 after dominating High-A Spokane, is considered the most advanced of the group and could be a candidate for a big-league opportunity as early as 2027. The Rockies' rotation posted a 6.65 ERA in 2025, the highest in baseball's modern era dating to 1901, underscoring the urgency of developing homegrown pitching.

weather.notable: Nearly 6 degrees above the average high for this time of year.

joke.joke: What is a date really, but a job interview that lasts all night?

topStory.headline: Iran Strikes Tanker in Strait of Hormuz, Attacks Gulf Bases as U.S. Enters 10th Night of Airstrikes
topStory.summary:
Iran attacked a commercial tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, forcing its crew to abandon the vessel and board a lifeboat, according to the United Kingdom's Maritime Trade Operations center. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed responsibility for that attack as well as two additional ship strikes in the waterway on Monday. The IRGC also said it targeted U.S. military sites including air defense systems and a radar installation in Bahrain and Kuwait, prompting air alert sirens in Bahrain and drawing intercept responses from Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan.

The Pentagon confirmed that nearly 100 U.S. service members have been injured over the past two weeks of escalating conflict, though spokesperson Sean Parnell said 96 percent had returned to duty after sustaining mostly minor concussions. Three U.S. soldiers were killed in action in Jordan and Iraq over the weekend, prompting President Trump to warn on social media that Iran would "pay for that killing many times over." The U.S. military said its 10th consecutive night of airstrikes targeted Iranian military command centers and missile and drone launch sites to degrade Tehran's ability to attack commercial shipping. Iranian media reported strikes on Bandar Abbas, Tabriz and Bushehr, which hosts Iran's only operational civilian nuclear power plant.

Diplomatic efforts to end the fighting continued Tuesday as Iran's Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni traveled to Islamabad for closed-door meetings with Pakistani Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir and was expected to meet Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. Pakistan played a central role in brokering a June memorandum of understanding between Washington and Tehran, but that interim ceasefire has collapsed as hostilities intensified over the past week. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Sunday the United States remained open to negotiations with Iran but that any deal "has to be real."

The broader regional picture deteriorated further Monday when Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthi movement announced an immediate maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea, citing the kingdom's decade-long blockade of Yemen. The Houthis said the Bab al-Mandeb strait, through which roughly 12 percent of global trade normally passes, would be closed to Saudi vessels. Saudi Arabia said it would take all necessary measures to protect its ships under international law, though the threat adds pressure on Riyadh's oil exports at a time when Hormuz shipping has largely stalled. Brent crude eased slightly to $88.87 a barrel in early Tuesday trading after reaching its highest level in more than a month in the previous session.
